title: ProofVerificationResult
description: Response from POST /v2/proofs/:id/verify. Returned to SDK callers of client.verifyProof(proofId) and reused by the offline CLI (npx @atlasent/verify proof.json) so online + offline paths produce byte-identical output. 'valid' means every check passed; 'invalid' means at least one check failed; 'incomplete' means the proof is well-formed but still pending (e.g. execution_status='pending' with no consumed_at).

verification_status.description: Top-level status. 'valid' iff every item in `checks` passed. 'incomplete' is reserved for proofs whose consume step has not yet landed (async execution); callers can poll rather than treating it as a hard failure.

proof_id.description: Echo of the proof id that was verified. Present so batch verifiers can correlate results without reordering the input.

signing_key_id.description: Registry id of the Ed25519 key the signature verified under. Absent when verification_status='invalid' due to invalid_signature or retired_signing_key.

audit_hash.description: Echo of Proof.audit_hash. Lets callers correlate a verify result with an audit_events row without fetching the full proof.

payload_hash.description: Echo of Proof.payload_hash so callers can cross-check client-side hashPayload() output before trusting the verdict.

checks.description: Per-check results. Identical set of check names across online and offline verifiers; order is canonical (see check_names in the $defs below).

name.description: Canonical check name. New checks appended here are opt-in — older SDKs ignore unknown check names rather than treat them as failures.

reason.description: Machine-readable failure reason. Required when passed=false, absent when passed=true. SDKs surface this verbatim so customers can wire alerts on specific codes.
